title: FortiSASE ZTNA Application Access (Fortinet)
spl:
index=zt sourcetype="fgt_traffic" subtype="forward" earliest=-24h
| where isnotnull(ztna_tag) OR isnotnull(ztna_rule)
| stats count dc(user) as users by ztna_tag, dstip, action
| sort -count
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.action All_Traffic.dest span=1h
description: FortiSASE ZTNA tags replace traditional VPN by granting per-application access based on device posture and identity. Monitoring ZTNA tag matches and application access patterns validates zero-trust enforcement.
value: FortiSASE ZTNA tags replace traditional VPN by granting per-application access based on device posture and identity. Monitoring ZTNA tag matches and application access patterns validates zero-trust enforcement.
implementation: Map `ztna_tag` to EMS posture profiles. Alert when ZTNA deny rate exceeds baseline (posture drift or misconfigured tags). Report on ZTNA adoption vs legacy VPN connections.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE ZTNA logs).
â€˘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=fgt_traffic` (FortiSASE ZTNA sessions).
â€˘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
Map `ztna_tag` to EMS posture profiles. Alert when ZTNA deny rate exceeds baseline (posture drift or misconfigured tags). Report on ZTNA adoption vs legacy VPN connections.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=zt sourcetype="fgt_traffic" subtype="forward" earliest=-24h
| where isnotnull(ztna_tag) OR isnotnull(ztna_rule)
| stats count dc(user) as users by ztna_tag, dstip, action
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**FortiSASE ZTNA Application Access (Fortinet)** â€” FortiSASE ZTNA tags replace traditional VPN by granting per-application access based on device posture and identity. Monitoring ZTNA tag matches and application access patterns validates zero-trust enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=fgt_traffic` (FortiSASE ZTNA sessions). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE ZTNA logs). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: zt; **sourcetype**: fgt_traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€˘ Scopes the data: index=zt, sourcetype="fgt_traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
â€˘ Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(ztna_tag) OR isnotnull(ztna_rule)` â€” typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
â€˘ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ztna_tag, dstip, action** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
â€˘ Orders rows with `sort` â€” combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.action All_Traffic.dest span=1h
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**FortiSASE ZTNA Application Access (Fortinet)** â€” FortiSASE ZTNA tags replace traditional VPN by granting per-application access based on device posture and identity. Monitoring ZTNA tag matches and application access patterns validates zero-trust enforcement.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=fgt_traffic` (FortiSASE ZTNA sessions). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE ZTNA logs). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€˘ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` â€” enable acceleration for that model.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 â€” Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (ZTNA tag access), Bar chart (actions by tag), Line chart (ZTNA sessions trend), Single value (ZTNA vs VPN ratio).
visualization: Table (ZTNA tag access), Bar chart (actions by tag), Line chart (ZTNA sessions trend), Single value (ZTNA vs VPN ratio).
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands â€” correlate with change management windows.
hardware: FortiSASE (cloud), FortiClient (EMS-managed endpoint), FortiGate ZTNA proxy
